124201.explanation: What matters is whether you are qualified for the job. Men or women should not receive extra priority solely for diversity reasons.

124202.explanation: We support collecting information from residents. Unfortunately, the proposal for a referendum regulation did not pass in the council on December 16, 2025.

124205.explanation: Rules were drawn up in consultation with hospitality entrepreneurs and residents.

124206.explanation: Good small-scale initiatives deserve support, with the municipality acting as a facilitator and stimulator.

124207.explanation: New centers can only be allowed within the current zoning plans. They can also contribute to local employment and economic vitality.

124208.explanation: We do not want an asylum seeker center (AZC) in Stichtse Vecht.

124210.explanation: A good wood stove causes no nuisance. We oppose a ban on the use of fireplaces and wood-burning stoves.

124215.explanation: Entrepreneurs themselves are responsible for complying with legal accessibility requirements.

124217.explanation: A local broadcaster is the watchdog of local democracy.

124222.explanation: Camera surveillance can contribute to safety and enforcement, but cameras should only be installed where necessary.

124223.explanation: There must be direct and safe shelter available for residents who need it most at that moment.

124224.explanation: Fortunately, we do not yet have any municipality where a baton is required for a BOA (special enforcement officer) to properly perform their duties. A permit for this must first be requested by the municipality, and BOAs must be trained in the use of a baton.

124226.explanation: We are in favor of giving as much priority as possible to young people and elderly from Stichtse Vecht when allocating social rental housing.

124229.explanation: It is important to counteract vacancy. If offices are no longer needed, they should be converted into housing.

124231.explanation: Where possible, this can also provide opportunities for reintegration and participation, but with regard to personal circumstances.

124232.explanation: This is a legal instrument to write off municipal taxes in the case of residents with low incomes. It is applied to residents who have, so to speak, really hit rock bottom.

124233.explanation: We find the current income limit to be appropriate.

124234.explanation: We do not think more money needs to be spent on youth work, but from our perspective cuts are also unnecessary.

124235.explanation: There are sufficient parking spaces designated for electric cars.

124236.explanation: Reduce the maximum speed in all village centers to 30 km per hour.

124237.explanation: We do not want paid parking in Stichtse Vecht and therefore no parking permits.

124239.explanation: Good care is independent and equally accessible to everyone. Care must be central, not religion, culture, or beliefs.

124240.explanation: Price is only one of the aspects to consider. Quality, performance, and capacity should weigh equally, perhaps even more, than price. So price/quality ratios must be carefully balanced.

124241.explanation: These resources are perfectly suitable for arranging very small amounts by oneself.

124244.explanation: Expand where possible and where there is demand. This keeps space for entrepreneurship and jobs, with attention to careful spatial planning.

124246.explanation: Give priority to residents who have been on the waiting list for years when allocating housing.

124248.explanation: First realize solar panels on roofs.

124249.explanation: School swimming is important for the safety of children. It can prevent drowning accidents and provides equal opportunities for all children.

124251.explanation: Stichtse Vecht currently has 30% social housing. This has been the case for years and is higher than the national average. This is fine.

124255.explanation: NO! No wind turbines anywhere in Stichtse Vecht!

124256.explanation: We find it important to be in contact with residents and are always reachable, both by phone and email.

124257.explanation: We want to protect our vulnerable nature.

124258.explanation: Agreed! But it is also important to build for the elderly so that the flow from large to small housing keeps moving.

124201.explanation: We realize this is not always achievable with an odd number of aldermen, but a balanced representation should be an aspiration.

124202.explanation: We consider a referendum too simplistic a tool to address complex problems.

124205.explanation: This depends on the environment and the local residents.

124208.explanation: We are not fundamentally for or against an asylum seekers' center (AZC). We have a preference for small-scale shelter.

124210.explanation: This depends on where you live and weather conditions. We prefer to follow the advice of the RIVM (National Institute for Public Health and the Environment).

124215.explanation: It is primarily the responsibility of the entrepreneur. Small entrepreneurs with an important social function could possibly be financially supported by the municipality.

124217.explanation: The local broadcaster is already financially supported. If costs increase, a discussion about a higher municipal contribution is possible.

124222.explanation: This is only necessary where it demonstrably adds value.

124223.explanation: In this way, our municipality can contribute to the shelter of women and men and their children in unsafe situations.

124224.explanation: As long as the BOAs indicate it is not necessary, we find the baton counterproductive. The monopoly on violence lies with the police. If BOAs feel unsafe, we must look at means to support them.

124237.explanation: This is a measure to reduce parking pressure in certain overloaded neighborhoods (around stations). It is also important to make more space for cyclists and pedestrians. It is especially important to impose restrictions on households and businesses with more than two cars.

124239.explanation: Preferably yes, but this cannot be guaranteed.

124240.explanation: Quality is more important than price.

124241.explanation: Especially for those who need it financially and preferably through the food bank. The municipality should facilitate this more.

124243.explanation: Tourists should also contribute to municipal facilities.

124244.explanation: We must use scarce land sparingly. We consider housing more important than business parks.

124246.explanation: This is a legal obligation. Our municipality must properly implement the distribution law. In practice, less than 10% of social rental homes go to status holders.

124248.explanation: It must remain limited. We strongly support a good mix of solar and wind energy.

124249.explanation: We consider it vital that every child in the Netherlands can swim, but this does not necessarily have to be through schools. Therefore, the municipality must allocate funds for swimming lessons, especially for residents who have no experience with open water.

124251.explanation: Only 22% of the housing in Stichtse Vecht is social rental housing, and the waiting time for a social rental home is over twelve years.

124255.explanation: The municipality must start an honest discussion with residents about the pros and cons of wind turbines. The municipality should be objective.

124201.explanation: It is about the quality of our administrators, not gender. A good balance with diverse backgrounds and competencies contributes to a strong municipal government.

124202.explanation: A referendum reduces difficult decisions to a simple yes-no question. In politics, choices must always be made amid scarcity. Not only in terms of financial resources but also space. For this, good and intensive citizen participation is a better tool than a referendum.

124205.explanation: Cafes and restaurants may set their own opening hours, as long as they coordinate with the neighborhood. Residents and entrepreneurs are mature enough to make agreements among themselves. The municipality may only intervene in cases of safety or nuisance risks.

124206.explanation: The municipality does not facilitate private initiatives. Ultimately, generating sustainable energy is a way to reduce energy costs together. It is not necessary for the municipality to step in for this. However, the municipality can support the start (a study) of an initiative.

124207.explanation: There is no place in Stichtse Vecht for large block buildings that offer no added value. We want to provide space for companies that stimulate employment and innovation.

124208.explanation: The VVD is not in favor of an asylum seekers' center in Stichtse Vecht. Such a center is too large for the absorption capacity of our villages. However, a possible satellite of an existing asylum seekers' center (maximum 75 people) could be an option in Stichtse Vecht.

124210.explanation: The use of fireplaces and wood stoves is allowed when there is sufficient wind. It is important to educate residents about the health risks on windless days for other neighbors. Banning is not an end in itself.

124215.explanation: This is the responsibility of building owners. The municipality must set a good example by ensuring municipal properties are well accessible to people with disabilities.

124217.explanation: When the local broadcaster initiates new projects that add value to community cohesion, it is possible they receive additional resources beyond existing funds. Concrete result agreements must then be made.

124222.explanation: When unsafe situations occur, the municipality should not hesitate to install camera surveillance. Camera surveillance is an important tool to keep public spaces safe.

124223.explanation: It is important that there are enough places where people can stay safely when faced with aggression and violence. These do not necessarily have to be in Stichtse Vecht.

124224.explanation: If the community enforcement officers (boa's) want to have batons or other protective equipment themselves, the municipality must facilitate this. It is important that boa's can perform their work safely.

124226.explanation: It is important to give priority to people with local ties. This not only concerns people living in the municipality but also, for example, people who have previously lived here.

124229.explanation: No subsidy from the municipality is needed for this. In consultation with owners, it is quite possible to reach a financially feasible development without the municipality adding extra money.

124231.explanation: Residents receiving social assistance benefits must contribute to society in other ways according to their capacity. This keeps them involved and ensures they also give something back.

124232.explanation: It is important that residents with a low income are not put in further trouble. The VVD does not want to increase this group. That means people up to the social assistance level receive this waiver.

124233.explanation: The VVD believes this income threshold should be lowered. This way, it becomes more attractive to work. If the income threshold is raised, the incentive to work decreases further.

124234.explanation: Cutting the youth work budget is unnecessary. Youth work must be used effectively to ultimately prevent more expensive care for (vulnerable) youths.

124235.explanation: Many spots are already designated for electric cars. First, it must be checked if people can charge their electric cars on their own property. When there is actual demand, installing a charging station makes sense.

124236.explanation: This is already the case in most places in the municipality. It is important that infrastructure is also adapted accordingly. It is also important to continuously monitor good traffic flow to travel quickly from A to B.

124237.explanation: The parking problem in Stichtse Vecht is not severe enough to introduce permit parking. This is very costly and also causes a lot of administrative burden for residents, entrepreneurs, and the municipality itself.

124239.explanation: Due to scarcity in healthcare, meeting this demand is not feasible. Waiting times are sometimes already too long. It is especially important that care is provided quickly, appropriately, and well.

124240.explanation: It is crucial to make good agreements about how care is provided. Good, appropriate, and prompt care is important. This way, bigger problems can be prevented. It is especially important not to prematurely purchase long and expensive care trajectories.

124241.explanation: It is important to see what is needed to help people make ends meet. This could be, for example, better guidance to work for residents, so this support is unnecessary. If such an initiative existed, the national government should play a role here.

124243.explanation: The tourist tax in Stichtse Vecht is at a normal level compared to surrounding municipalities. Stichtse Vecht is an attractive place to stay. It is also important that visitors contribute to, for example, the maintenance of roads, greenery, etc.

124244.explanation: It is important to look at more space for entrepreneurs. At the same time, it is essential to carefully consider which businesses we want to attract to Stichtse Vecht. For the VVD, these are entrepreneurs who increase employment and innovation.

124246.explanation: This is already arranged in Stichtse Vecht and will remain so according to the VVD.

124248.explanation: Banning solar panels on farmland is not possible. When there is a local initiative from residents or entrepreneurs who want to install solar panels, it must be considered. Solar panels cannot be placed in areas with high natural value.

124249.explanation: This is not a municipal task according to the VVD.

124251.explanation: According to the VVD, we should focus on affordable owner-occupied homes rather than more social rental homes, so that starting families can also buy a home in Stichtse Vecht.

124254.explanation: It is important that enforcement officers are visible to residents and entrepreneurs.

124255.explanation: The municipality must continue to oppose the placement of wind turbines-not only at Vreeland but everywhere in and around the municipality. For example, the wind turbines that might be placed at Lage Weide.

124256.explanation: It is always important to be accessible and approachable. Any initiative that contributes to this will receive VVD support. Better accessibility is different from increasing the opportunities to speak during political meetings.

124257.explanation: This is not a primary task of municipal enforcement, as it comes at the expense of other enforcement trajectories that deserve higher priority, such as the construction of illegal buildings.

124258.explanation: It is particularly important to build affordable owner-occupied homes in Stichtse Vecht. Many starters and young families quickly lose eligibility for social rental housing. At present, there is no place for them in Stichtse Vecht because there are too few affordable owner-occupied homes.

124201.explanation: The starting point is a good reflection of society.

124202.explanation: D66 Stichtse Vecht is not in favor of a referendum for the sake of the referendum, but does support the principle that residents from the community can put forward subjects that affect the entire municipality and submit them to all residents.

124205.explanation: This is done in consultation with residents, the municipality, and other entrepreneurs.

124206.explanation: D66 chooses to be facilitative towards projects that are future-proof and appropriate for our municipality. Therefore, there are no automatic subsidies.

124207.explanation: D66 chooses to locate large companies on business parks along the A2.

124208.explanation: We adhere to the legal agreements for refugee shelter, preferring small-scale housing spread throughout the municipality, always with clear quality standards, resident participation, and opportunities for refugees to actively take part in society during their stay.

124210.explanation: D66 does not choose to ban wood burning, but there should be more focus on awareness.

124217.explanation: We have a local broadcaster and currently see no need to provide additional support.

124222.explanation: More camera surveillance is not automatic; where necessary, camera surveillance is deployed.

124223.explanation: It is good that these houses exist; they do not necessarily have to be within the municipality’s own borders.

124224.explanation: This is a national discussion, but currently there are no reasons to pursue this in our municipality.

124226.explanation: D66 chooses to give all housing seekers who want to stay in our municipality priority for social rental housing.

124229.explanation: We accelerate procedures, make binding agreements with housing corporations and market parties, and ensure a balanced mix of homes. However, we do not provide subsidies upfront.

124231.explanation: D66 chooses to support and stimulate so that everyone can participate in society.

124232.explanation: The threshold for qualifying for remission is already high.

124233.explanation: We opt for help based on trust and for prevention: rather preventing problems than repairing them afterward. We already have flexible boundaries for all residents.

124234.explanation: As D66, we are in favor of addressing problems early on.

124235.explanation: Residents can request charging spots themselves when there are too few charging points.

124236.explanation: Mobility priorities: 30 km/h roads where cars and bicycles share the same road.

124237.explanation: Parking permits are a means, such as at Breukelen station, not an end in themselves.

124239.explanation: D66 assumes that care providers and those in need of care jointly look for the most appropriate care.

124240.explanation: D66 chooses to primarily focus on quality; that is investing in a sustainable future.

124241.explanation: This is one example of poverty that is often invisible; the municipality must provide help for residents who need it.

124243.explanation: The current level is not a problem. If we lower it, we must raise other taxes for residents.

124244.explanation: A good example is Schulp’s relocation from the dirt path to a business park. Of course, space must then be made for that.

124246.explanation: We meet the legal obligations for housing status holders and ensure they not only receive suitable housing but also the opportunities to integrate and actively participate in our society.

124248.explanation: Space for the energy transition – We cleverly plan space for energy generation, storage, and transport. Large-scale energy infrastructure belongs in the 'Snelle Vecht,' the part of the municipality where there is room for industry, business activity, infrastructure, and more intensive land use.

124249.explanation: That is the responsibility of the national government. Swimming lessons are paid via the U-PAS.

124251.explanation: D66 opts for a balanced mix (at least 30% social rental) tailored to the needs per village and aimed at opportunities for starters and suitable for seniors.

124254.explanation: Enforcement is partly based on signals from residents. Random visits do not contribute to a safer environment.

124255.explanation: For the Garstenpolder and Garstenhoek, we continue to exert influence on the province to prevent wind turbines from being placed there and to preserve the area's small-scale, landscape, and cultural-historical character.

124256.explanation: Council members are easily reachable through various channels, but many residents still experience a distance. More information is needed for this.

124257.explanation: Waste is regularly dumped in ditches and drains. This must be properly enforced.

124258.explanation: We have community ties throughout the municipality, meaning that starters, young families, and people in need of social housing from the villages have priority.

124201.explanation: Streekbelangen stands for equal opportunities and a good male-female balance in the executive board. Diversity is important, but aldermen are appointed based on quality and support, not on mandatory quotas.

124202.explanation: Resident participation is essential: residents must be involved early and have influence through consultation, dialogue, and participation processes. Complex issues cannot be reduced to a yes/no choice and require nuance. That is why the party is not in favor of referendums.

124205.explanation: Streekbelangen is generally positive about giving hospitality entrepreneurs the freedom to determine their opening hours. However, attention must remain for livability, safety, and nuisance for residents.

124206.explanation: Streekbelangen is positive about financial support for joint, local, sustainable energy initiatives. It is important that the proceeds from energy generation benefit residents and the community locally as much as possible.

124207.explanation: Streekbelangen supports economic growth but remains critical. New distribution centers often cause extra traffic, landscape disruption, and limited local added value. Livability and spatial quality come first.

124208.explanation: Sheltering asylum seekers requires solidarity, but decisions must have local support, fit the scale of the municipality, and be made in good consultation with residents.

124210.explanation: Streekbelangen believes that the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should, in principle, remain allowed. However, it is important to consider health and nuisance; good education and responsible use are preferred over a general ban.

124215.explanation: Streekbelangen is positive about this. Accessibility is important; the municipality can support entrepreneurs with financial aid or arrangements to make buildings more usable.

124217.explanation: Streekbelangen is positive about this. A strong local broadcaster is important for information and democracy; additional support is possible if transparent, targeted, and fitting within a responsible municipal budget.

124222.explanation: Streekbelangen is positive about the use of camera surveillance in certain specific cases. Targeted and temporary camera surveillance can contribute to safety in problem areas, provided it is carefully considered and respects privacy.

124223.explanation: Protection of victims of domestic violence is essential. However, regional cooperation, need, and feasibility must be examined to ensure effective and well-organized assistance.

124224.explanation: Safe working conditions are important, but de-escalation and good cooperation with the police are preferred over further arming.

124226.explanation: Local ties may play a role in the allocation of social rental housing so that residents of Stichtse Vecht have more opportunities.

124229.explanation: Converting vacant offices into homes can help address the housing shortage; municipal subsidies are possible if used purposefully and financially responsibly.

124231.explanation: Participation and activation are important, but social work should be appropriate and supportive where possible-not mandatory or punitive.

124232.explanation: Waiving municipal taxes is important to support residents with low incomes and prevent financial problems.

124233.explanation: Streekbelangen takes a nuanced position here. It is open to relaxation if it turns out residents otherwise fall through the cracks, provided it is financially responsible.

124234.explanation: Streekbelangen is not in favor of cutting youth work. Youth work is important for prevention, guidance, and opportunities for young people; investing now prevents greater social costs later.

124235.explanation: More charging points are needed for the energy transition, provided this is done carefully and does not increase general parking pressure; customization per locality is important.

124236.explanation: Traffic safety is important, but a general 30 km/h limit is not an end in itself; customization per street and locality is needed, tailored to the situation and local support.

124237.explanation: Parking permits are not an end in themselves and may only be introduced where parking pressure is demonstrably high, with attention to livability, affordability, and support in the neighborhood.

124239.explanation: Freedom of choice and appropriate care are important; where possible, care should align with a person's faith, culture, or worldview. But this must fit within quality, availability, and affordability limits.

124240.explanation: In youth care, quality and continuity must come first; price is important but should not lead at the expense of good and appropriate care for children and families.

124241.explanation: Free menstrual products can help prevent poverty and exclusion, provided they are targeted (e.g., via schools and social locations) and financially responsible.

124243.explanation: Streekbelangen takes a nuanced position here. Tourism is important for the local economy; a lower tourist tax can help but must be balanced with municipal costs and residents' livability.

124244.explanation: Expanding business parks may be necessary for local employment, but only if done carefully, fitting the environment, with attention to accessibility, sustainability, and livability.

124246.explanation: Permit holders have a right to housing, but priority must not be at the expense of other housing seekers.

124248.explanation: Sustainable energy is important, but solar panels on agricultural land are not an end in themselves. Landscape and agriculture protection weigh heavily; customization and alternatives are preferred.

124249.explanation: School swimming contributes to safety and equal opportunities for children; municipal support is desirable.

124251.explanation: More social rental housing is necessary, as well as affordable owner-occupied homes. A fixed percentage of 35% is not an end in itself. Streekbelangen opts for customization per location, fitting needs, affordability, and housing flow.

124254.explanation: Visible enforcers in neighborhoods increase safety and trust; their presence should focus on contact, prevention, and signaling problems, not just enforcement.

124255.explanation: Sustainable energy is important, but Streekbelangen believes these wind turbines do not fit the landscape and living environment and considers the impact on residents and nature unacceptable.

124256.explanation: An accessible and approachable municipal council is essential for local democracy; residents should find it easy to contact them and be taken seriously.

124257.explanation: Clean water is important for nature, recreation, and health; stricter and targeted enforcement against water pollution is needed to prevent violations.

124258.explanation: Housing construction must meet the needs of starters, young families, and those dependent on social rental housing, with attention to a balanced and fair distribution of homes.

124201.explanation: Quality is more important than gender.

124202.explanation: Difficult problems cannot be solved with a simple yes or no.

124205.explanation: We have a harmonized hospitality policy, in which closing times are well regulated. We want to keep it that way to prevent nuisance and other problems.

124206.explanation: We do not see this as a task of the municipality, especially given all the financial challenges your municipality already faces.

124207.explanation: In some locations, this could work well and would also provide employment.

124208.explanation: We prefer small-scale shelters over large asylum seeker centers.

124210.explanation: We do hope residents follow the wood stove guide. We see no possibility to enforce this statement.

124215.explanation: We believe this is the responsibility of the entrepreneur.

124217.explanation: We believe that currently enough money goes to RTV Stichtse Vecht, and otherwise we are sure to hear that from this local broadcaster.

124222.explanation: Provided there is a good reason for it.

124223.explanation: If a request comes to the municipality, we are in favor of it, but at the moment we do not see that this is necessary or desirable.

124224.explanation: The BOAs (special enforcement officers) have few resources and we want to enable this.

124226.explanation: We believe in core community ties, as is already working well now.

124229.explanation: This does not need to be subsidized, because companies can apply a business model to it. We do believe that offices should be converted into housing.

124231.explanation: In many cases, this is not feasible.

124232.explanation: That will only lead to more costs in other areas if people cannot pay the bill(s).

124233.explanation: We have currently set a limit that fits with the national approach. We want to keep it that way and follow national policy in this.

124234.explanation: We think this would lead to major unrest if it were to happen.

124235.explanation: Currently, enough spots have been created, partly due to the efforts of the CDA in recent years.

124236.explanation: The road must be designed in such a way that this speed is logical.

124237.explanation: Absolutely not in favor of it.

124239.explanation: We believe the staff shortage is so great that you can make this request, but unfortunately it often cannot be honored.

124240.explanation: Quality is more important than price.

124241.explanation: There are already several places where this can be done for free, and we think it is good that the municipality is committed to this.

124243.explanation: It is already low in Stichtse Vecht, and last year it was actually adjusted to market conditions. We want to keep it that way.

124244.explanation: Only if it fits within the surroundings.

124246.explanation: There are fixed agreements about that. Others also urgently need housing.

124248.explanation: Banning is not necessary; it should be discouraged. If a farmer really wants this, it must be possible.

124249.explanation: This is not feasible at all in various villages, and there is hardly any demand from most schools.

124251.explanation: We find 30% sufficient, as we do now.

124254.explanation: It is good to be visible in all neighborhoods and villages of Stichtse Vecht. In many neighborhoods, we have seen enforcers walking more often in recent years.

124255.explanation: This does not suit the environment. First all roofs full, then our cultural-historical area.

124256.explanation: We notice that residents do know how to find us well if they delve into it.

124257.explanation: We have no indications that there are problems in this area requiring more enforcement.

124258.explanation: There are various groups of housing seekers who deserve priority, like these, but there are several more to consider. We especially want to give priority to people from our own neighborhoods.

124201.explanation: You want the best people for the job. Whether they are men or women.

124202.explanation: We are not in favor of a referendum ordinance because we believe that referenda do not contribute to a qualitatively good decision-making process.

124205.explanation: It is better to regulate this at the municipal level.

124206.explanation: It is important to develop policy on the conditions under which this takes place.

124207.explanation: Provided traffic flow and parking regulation are well arranged for the area. Too much can become visually dominant.

124208.explanation: Sufficient shelter must be provided, whether it has to be an asylum seekers’ center (AZC) is the question. The municipality must comply with legal obligations. Support among residents must be brought to the attention of national politics.

124210.explanation: It is important that good information is provided about the adverse effects.

124215.explanation: Inclusion is important; entrepreneurs primarily have responsibility in this themselves.

124217.explanation: It is important that this is properly arranged at the national level. Independent media must not depend on politics. Meanwhile, we live in a world where disinformation is increasing. Reliable journalism and local broadcasters are important in this.

124222.explanation: More is not always better; it must be done efficiently.

124223.explanation: Providing protection for the vulnerable is very important.

124224.explanation: BOAs must act effectively during their work when confronted with aggression and violence. Sadly, society is becoming more hardened. It is important that BOAs have the resources to properly perform their work, where de-escalation should remain the norm.

124226.explanation: Now people get priority in their own neighborhood. This is to the disadvantage of neighborhoods with little social housing. We would like to see this broadened from neighborhood to municipality.

124229.explanation: If municipal subsidy is needed for this, it can also be repaid when the investments have proven profitable.

124231.explanation: If possible, that is a nice reciprocal gesture. The possibilities for this are expected to be limited because there is a reason why these people are not working.

124232.explanation: It would be better if people with low incomes have enough room to pay all fixed expenses. Unfortunately, that is not yet the case, so waiving debts is a good solution to support those on minimum incomes.

124233.explanation: Help to prevent poverty can never come too early.

124234.explanation: This will not contribute to the development of young people. Meanwhile, it is important to look at how the money is spent.

124235.explanation: There will be more and more electric vehicles; it is important that they can also be charged somewhere.

124236.explanation: This really depends on the location.

124237.explanation: Not as a source of income. For some areas, it is important that residents can park their cars near their homes again.

124239.explanation: In our election program it states: Interpret care in the broadest sense and guarantee freedom of choice for care that corresponds to one's beliefs.

124240.explanation: In our election program it states: Improve the quality and alignment of youth care with identity and needs; do not purchase solely based on price.

124241.explanation: Not a primary task of the municipality. However, it is important that everyone can participate.

124243.explanation: Does not necessarily have to increase.

124244.explanation: As long as it fits within the zoning plan and takes residents into account.

124246.explanation: The municipality must carry out its legal task. If this happens through the urgency system as we know it, this seems to do justice to the situation.

124248.explanation: Yes, if the soil is fertile enough for planting.

124249.explanation: School swimming is an enormous investment for schools beyond the money. It is important that everyone learns to swim. If the costs are the problem, fortunately there are arrangements to support people with this.

124251.explanation: In the housing vision, the standard is established based on needs research. This is not a political free choice. If more is needed, the housing vision must be adjusted.

124254.explanation: It is important that enforcement officers have enough time to go into the neighborhoods.

124255.explanation: We are against the placement of wind turbines in our municipality.

124256.explanation: The council has many opportunities to speak out or contact council members.

124257.explanation: When it comes to surface water, we find it important that it is well protected.

124258.explanation: Building is important in every form.

124201.explanation: Candidates must above all be competent administrators. For us, inclusion and diversity come first. Not necessarily a man or a woman.

124202.explanation: In the council meeting of December 16 last year, we advocated for the introduction of an advisory and consultative referendum. We are great supporters of citizen participation in all its forms.

124205.explanation: Agree, provided it is in good consultation with the environment and that they also take care of proper order enforcement themselves.

124206.explanation: Agreed that citizen initiatives should be financially supported to increase their chances of success. However, those funds can also come from other subsidy programs than local ones, such as provincial and national governments and tax options.

124207.explanation: The space is very limited and must be used beneficially. Additionally, distribution centers cause more traffic pressure and do not provide good employment for local residents.

124208.explanation: In our municipality, there would be room for small-scale shelter, like what we currently realize at Planetenbaan. So no large-scale shelter.

124210.explanation: We are still neutral on this.

124215.explanation: The Social Shelter Act requires that everyone must be able to participate. Now only adjustments in the domestic sphere are reimbursed. As far as we are concerned, a subsidy scheme may be established to implement the UN Disability Convention.

124217.explanation: The municipality does that too; we receive a national contribution which we then pass on to the local broadcaster. Local reporting is important.

124222.explanation: The municipal council does not decide on the use of cameras. That is a power of the mayor and the Public Prosecution Service. Unfortunately, due to increasing unrest and attacks, the municipality has to deploy more and more cameras. That is regrettable but necessary.

124223.explanation: In Stichtse Vecht, we have many families who get into trouble due to marital problems and partner violence, among other things. It would be nice if we could shelter our residents locally in a safe environment. Currently, Utrecht is the central municipality for us and carries out this task.

124224.explanation: BOAs (special enforcement officers) in Stichtse Vecht are equipped with a bodycam, baton, handcuffs, and pepper spray. Samen Stichtse Vecht supports de-escalating deployment but does not want BOAs to be sent out unprotected.

124226.explanation: That is arranged in the regional housing regulation and the specific local rules for the allocation policy. Certain professions and people with social ties are given priority.

124229.explanation: Certainly, but those are subsidies from the national government within the framework of the housing deal.

124231.explanation: People who receive social assistance benefits and fall under the Participation Act can be encouraged to perform informal care tasks or volunteer work. More importantly, we must challenge them to become part of society again. Poverty causes social isolation. More community centers.

124232.explanation: The government should never ask people for money when they are financially struggling. We should help them become financially resilient again. Moreover, collecting these amounts from people who cannot afford it will ultimately cost our society much more.

124233.explanation: We see more and more people facing financial hardship due to rising energy prices and living costs. The arrangements must be simpler and available to a larger group of residents.

124234.explanation: Precisely not. There is so little to do in the municipality, which means young people rely on the neighborhood. To guide young people, the deployment of youth workers is necessary.

124235.explanation: There are too few charging points available, while the vehicle fleet is electrifying. The municipality should also make it possible for private individuals to lay a cable duct so that a resident can charge at a public parking spot.

124236.explanation: In most places, we have already implemented that.

124237.explanation: We are in favor of facilitating more shared cars and bikes. In some areas, we may need to use blue zones to better regulate parking pressure. Paid parking or parking permits are not necessary for now, but possibly in the future.

124239.explanation: Residents with care needs must receive appropriate care. That is not always available, but it should be the starting point.

124240.explanation: They certainly need to consider the price as well, but quality, continuity, and availability are much more important.

124241.explanation: That already happens in certain places, the so-called MUPs. Samen Stichtse Vecht believes the municipality should not do this itself, but should encourage organizations to apply for and distribute such products through the poverty fund. The municipality has a role in promoting and facilitating.

124243.explanation: We already raised this tax significantly a few years ago, and now the national government is adding an extra levy. This means many people will no longer be able to vacation here, and only business tourism will be possible.

124244.explanation: The sites primarily need to be revitalized and modernized.

124246.explanation: In Stichtse Vecht, we do not give extra priority to permit holders. 30% of the social rental homes are reserved for urgent seekers, and within that 30%, 40% of the homes are allocated to permit holders. This ensures others also retain rights to urgency.

124248.explanation: Not banning, but encouraging especially large-scale installations on roofs, facades, bike paths, etc., while preserving agricultural lands as much as possible.

124249.explanation: Schools often do not want an additional task, which is why many have abolished school swimming lessons. The municipality offers the option for children from low-income families (U-pass holders) to attend free swimming lessons. We strongly support promoting swimming.

124251.explanation: At least 30% social housing is sufficient. We want mixed neighborhoods with affordable homes, so a mix of mid-rent and owner-occupied housing.

124254.explanation: BOAs are present, but residents say enforcement is insufficient. We want visible presence of BOAs and police on the streets because it has a preventive effect and makes enforcers more familiar with neighborhood residents.

124255.explanation: Absolutely no wind turbines in or near our municipality. We already suffer enough nuisance from the Amsterdam-Rhine Canal, the A2, air traffic, etc. We also have to resolve grid congestion here for Tenet and Stedin. You can't put all challenges on our municipality.

124256.explanation: Fortunately, we are often contacted and regularly speak with our residents. We do this as individual council members but also within the council itself. Luckily, our private phone numbers are still publicly available on the website. But we could organize committee meetings in the villages.

124257.explanation: Currently, this category is not classified as high risk, so no intensified inspections are necessary.

124258.explanation: If we build more for seniors, housing turnover can begin, allowing young families to take over homes from the elderly. Extra construction for starters is definitely needed, and that is happening extensively now in Maarssenbroek.

124202.explanation: We already submitted a motion on this in 2022, but it was rejected. A college proposal was again rejected in 2025 because several left-wing parties that supported it in 2022 now oppose it. PVV is unconditionally in favor of a referendum.

124205.explanation: Provided they do not cause nuisance.

124206.explanation: The municipality should stay far away from left-wing hobbies.

124208.explanation: No asylum seeker centers; this must be prevented by all legal means.

124210.explanation: Please let the residents decide this themselves.

124215.explanation: It is an obligation of the entrepreneur.

124222.explanation: If you have nothing to hide, it doesn't matter. Images should be destroyed after a maximum of one week if the judiciary does not use them.

124226.explanation: Waiting times are already far too long.

124229.explanation: As long as they are given to our own residents.

124231.explanation: Only if they are physically capable of it.

124232.explanation: As long as it is checked that payment is truly impossible.

124233.explanation: A matter for the national government.

124234.explanation: The focus should be on enforcement against offenders.

124235.explanation: Electrification of the fleet is going too far.

124236.explanation: Where it can be done safely, higher speeds are allowed.

124237.explanation: There must be sufficient parking spaces.

124239.explanation: If a man refuses care from a woman, that is his own responsibility.

124241.explanation: Only for single mothers on welfare.

124244.explanation: If there is room and enough applicants.

124246.explanation: Refugees already cost us more than enough, and why should they have priority over someone who has been on the housing list for years?

124248.explanation: This is basically a matter for the farmer, but the PVV is not in favor of solar panels on agricultural land.

124254.explanation: If you take enforcement seriously, yes.

124255.explanation: Subsidized wind turbines must never be allowed in Stichtse Vecht.

124256.explanation: The PVV is always accessible to the residents.

124258.explanation: Priority should be given to starters with an essential profession.

statements.title: The municipal council of Stichtse Vecht should be more accessible and accountable to residents.
Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The municipality should agree to the placement of wind turbines near Vreeland.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet instemmen met de plaatsing van

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

statements.title: Stichtse Vecht should install more CCTV/camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: Stichtse Vecht should introduce a parking-permit system in more neighborhoods.

titleParts.text: Stichtse Vecht moet in meer wijken een

titleParts.glossary: A permit to park in a certain place. You have to pay for that right.

statements.title: The BOAs who enforce public safety in Stichtse Vecht should be issued a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Stichtse Vecht controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Municipal enforcement officers should patrol the neighborhoods of Stichtse Vecht more often.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: moeten vaker de wijken van Stichtse Vecht in.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: Stichtse Vecht should establish a shelter for victims of domestic violence.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: Residents of Stichtse Vecht should be able to express their opinion on important issues through a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Stichtse Vecht moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should cut spending on youth work.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
People and organizations that help young people in the municipality with various things.

statements.title: The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be lowered.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: First-time buyers, young families and people in need of social housing should be given priority for housing.
The use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves should remain permitted in Stichtse Vecht.

titleParts.text: In Stichtse Vecht moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: Business parks in Stichtse Vecht may be expanded.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
At least 35% of newly built homes in Stichtse Vecht should be social housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Stichtse Vecht moet minimaal 35% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Enforcement should more strictly monitor water pollution.

shootoutStatements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
